skillMatrix.trigger: user requested a saved implementation plan and explicitly linked etrnl-dev-plan
skillMatrix.evidence: Plan follows the file-backed planning workflow and includes readiness artifact metadata.

skillMatrix.trigger: implementation will create and rename repo-owned skills
skillMatrix.evidence: Plan requires canonical skill directories, compact skill entrypoints, references, and prompt-budget checks.

skillMatrix.trigger: taxonomy rename and new audit categories risk prompt bloat and overlap
skillMatrix.evidence: Plan includes a simplifier review phase and rejects wholesale SkillsMP imports.

skillMatrix.trigger: new validators and audit categories may duplicate current code-health, docs-health, and deep-audit behavior
skillMatrix.evidence: Plan requires ownership-boundary decisions for excellence/code/repo and reuse category overlap.

skillMatrix.trigger: workflow architecture and naming taxonomy review
skillMatrix.evidence: Run during implementation if installed and relevant; planning artifact records architecture risks.

skillMatrix.trigger: planning artifact and mostly JavaScript/Markdown control-plane work
skillMatrix.evidence: No exported TypeScript contracts are planned; ordinary JS syntax and shell gates are required.
skillMatrix.notApplicableRationale: No exported/public TypeScript types, API contracts, runtime validation types, generated types, state machines, branded IDs, reusable type utilities, or cross-layer DTO/domain boundaries are touched by this plan.

decisions.reuseDecision: Extend the existing owned skill list and contract checker instead of adding a second registry.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: The flat naming scheme does not express family/category boundaries; enforcement must live in the existing source-of-truth path.

decisions.reuseDecision: Implement a clean rename by removing old repo-owned skill directories, command shims, docs references, and router paths after canonical replacements exist.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: Victor explicitly rejected old command preservation; the migration must not preserve old command behavior.

decisions.reuseDecision: Reuse the existing deep-audit registry and artifact validator for missing audit categories.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: UX, reuse, repo hygiene, and tooling need first-class categories but should not invent a parallel audit envelope.

findings.fix: Added a canonical etrnl-<family>-<capability> taxonomy with dev, audit, ops, and comm families.
Planned old-name removal and rejection gates instead of fallback shims or compatibility routing.
Mapped the seven audit prompts to canonical audit categories and separated existing coverage from missing categories.
Recorded SkillsMP material as scaffold evidence only and rejected wholesale imports.

completionAudit.planItem: write naming and audit suite plan
completionAudit.evidence: Plan file created with taxonomy, migration map, audit-category expansion, tests, rollback, and readiness sections.

reviewPhases.disposition: Plan solves the naming problem and audit expansion together, removes old commands instead of preserving aliases, and avoids wholesale third-party imports.
Plan is validator-first: taxonomy, old-name rejection fixtures, install sync, and deep-audit registry updates precede skill migration.
Canonical names group skills by operator intent; old names are removed rather than kept as aliases.
Main risks are command breakage, double routing, install drift, prompt bloat, category overlap, and dirty-worktree collision; each has a planned gate.
New categories must use compact entrypoints and rewritten references rather than large pasted prompt bodies.
The plan reuses the existing skill list, router, install, smoke, and deep-audit validator surfaces instead of adding parallel registries.
The naming scheme stays to four families and one command shape; category details remain in references and validators.

tddEvidence.rationaleWhenNotTestFirst: This turn writes a plan and readiness artifact only; the plan itself specifies red taxonomy, old-name rejection, router, and category fixture tests before implementation.

completionReconciliation.requestedOutcome: Create a better ETRNL naming scheme such as audit category names and dev flow names.
completionReconciliation.evidence: Plan defines etrnl-dev-*, etrnl-audit-*, etrnl-ops-*, and etrnl-comm-* families plus a full canonical mapping.

completionReconciliation.requestedOutcome: Plan how to turn the seven audit prompts and SkillsMP scaffolds into improved skills.
completionReconciliation.evidence: Plan maps each audit prompt to canonical audit skills and names missing categories to create.

completionReconciliation.requestedOutcome: Use etrnl-dev-plan for the plan-writing workflow.
completionReconciliation.evidence: Plan file and deep-stack artifact were created under docs/plans with readiness validation metadata.

reuseBindings.decision: Plan a canonical taxonomy and old-name removal layer using the existing registry/install/test machinery.
reuseBindings.newSurfaceJustification: The current flat naming scheme cannot express dev-flow versus audit-category skills clearly.

reuseBindings.decision: Plan missing audit categories in the existing deep-audit envelope.
reuseBindings.newSurfaceJustification: UX, reuse, repo hygiene, tooling, and possibly excellence are not first-class registered categories today.

sourceGate.evidence: This turn only creates a plan and artifact; source gates are listed for the implementation turn.

stagedInstall.evidence: No staged install requested during planning.

stagedDoctor.evidence: No staged doctor requested during planning.

rollbackVerification.evidence: Rollback verification applies after implementation or install.

liveInstallDecision.evidence: Live install requires explicit Victor approval.

riskTier.reason: Implementation will rename and add multiple repo-owned skill, docs, test, and validator surfaces but should not mutate live installed state without explicit approval.

typescript.ordinaryVerification: No TypeScript implementation is planned; JavaScript helpers use node --check and repo shell tests.

advancedTypes.notApplicableRationale: This plan does not touch advanced TypeScript surfaces.
